chapter_title.en: Chapter 46 – Inheritance of the lost (unknown whereabouts)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/231)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/231)
text: قَالَ لِي أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) الْمَفْقُودُ يُتَرَبَّصُ بِمَالِهِ أَرْبَعَ سِنِينَ ثُمَّ يُقْسَمُ .

en.hubeali: Yunus, from Is’haq Bin Ammar who said,
‘Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘The lost, one should wait with his wealth for four years, then distribute it’.
en.sarwar: 5. Yunus has narrated from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has narrated the following: “Abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, said to me, ‘The assets of a lost person must be kept for four years; then they are distributed.’”
